text:
Once upon a time in a quiet little town surrounded by mountains, there lived a humble cat named Luna. Luna loved birds. Especially starlings. Every evening, she'd sit perched on a branch somewhere high above the village, watching them weave through the trees.

On this particular day, something was wrong. The starlings didn’t seem themselves. None were chirping their usual songs. Luna tried calling out, but no response came. She scoured the sky with ears twitched like antennaes.

Not far from her favorite perch, a wise old owl sat upon a branch. Its eyes twinkled kindly. “Oh dear,” it exclaimed. “I’ve heard that a whirlwind from the north has snatched many of them.” The owl flicked its wing ominously.

Luna raced back to town. People were gathering. An invisible something was swooping down? A storm? Nothing like that. Then Luna spotted her flock. Not all were there. Some were fluttering around in
